yahya_birth.title: The birth of Yahya
yahya_birth.introduction: Yahya (يحيى عليه السلام), John the Baptist, is the son of the prophet Zakariya. The Quran says: "O Zakariya! We give you good news of a son. His name will be Yahya." (19:7) Yahya was born miraculously: his father was old and his mother barren. Allah fulfilled His promise.
yahya_birth.conclusion: Yahya was born as announced. His name means "he will live". He was a sign for the people. His birth confirmed that nothing is impossible for Allah.
yahya_birth.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that Allah's word is always fulfilled. Miracles remind us of the Creator's power.

chapters.title: The announcement of birth
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "We have not given before anyone of that name." (19:7) The name Yahya was unique. No one before him had borne it. It was a sign of divine election. Zakariya and his wife waited with faith. When the time came, Yahya was born. Family and relatives glorified Allah.

chapters.title: A blessed child
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And peace be upon him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he will be raised alive." (19:15) Yahya was blessed from birth. Peace (salam) was upon him at every stage of his life. He grew up in piety and wisdom. His destiny was to be a prophet.

references.content: يَـٰزَكَرِيَّآ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَـٰمٍ ٱسْمُهُۥ يَحْيَىٰ لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّۭا
references.translation: O Zakariya! We give you good news of a son. His name will be Yahya.
references.relevance: This verse relates the announcement of Yahya's birth.

references.content: وَسَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّۭا
references.translation: And peace upon him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he will be raised alive.
references.relevance: This verse establishes blessings upon Yahya.

glossary.definition: John in Arabic. Means "he will live". Prophet of purity.
Peace. Salam was upon Yahya at every stage.

yahya_purity.title: The purity of Yahya
yahya_purity.introduction: Yahya was a prophet of purity and piety. The Quran says: "And (Yahya) was dutiful to his parents. He was neither arrogant nor disobedient." (19:14) He lived in asceticism, far from worldly pleasures. He devoted himself entirely to the worship of Allah.
yahya_purity.conclusion: Yahya embodied purity. He never committed sin. He was a model for believers. His name is associated with chastity and piety.
yahya_purity.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that purity of heart and body is a virtue. The believer must stay away from evil and draw closer to Allah.

chapters.title: The ascetic life
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "And We gave him wisdom from childhood, and tenderness from Us and purity." (19:12-13) Yahya lived in the desert. He wore garments of hair. He ate simply. He sought neither wealth nor power. His only quest was Allah's pleasure.

chapters.title: Obedience
chapters.content: The Quran says: "He was neither arrogant nor disobedient." (19:14) Yahya obeyed his parents and Allah. He never rebelled. He committed no injustice. His life was an example of righteousness. People respected him for his piety.

references.content: يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّۭا ۝ وَحَنَانًۭا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةًۭ ۝ وَكَانَ تَقِيًّۭا
references.translation: O Yahya! Take the Book with strength. We gave him wisdom from childhood, tenderness and purity. He was pious.

references.content: مُصَدِّقًۢا بِكَلِمَةٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَسَيِّدًۭا وَحَصُورًۭا وَنَبِيًّۭا مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: Confirming a word from Allah, noble, chaste, and prophet among the righteous.

glossary.definition: Purity. Yahya received zakât (purity) from Allah.
Chaste. Yahya was hasur, abstinent from worldly pleasures.

yahya_isa.title: Yahya and the announcement of Isa
yahya_isa.introduction: Yahya and Isa (Jesus) are linked. The Quran says: "And (Yahya) confirmed a word from Allah." (3:39) Yahya announced the coming of Isa. He baptized people in the Jordan, preparing them to receive the message. He was the forerunner of the Messiah.
yahya_isa.conclusion: Yahya prepared the way for Isa. He testified to his coming. The two prophets met. Yahya recognized Isa as the promised Messiah. The message continued.
yahya_isa.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that prophets confirm each other. Each messenger prepares the way for the next. The prophetic chain is one.

chapters.title: The forerunner
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "(Yahya) confirmed a word from Allah." (3:39) Yahya called people to repent. He baptized them in the river. He announced the coming of one greater than himself. When Isa came to him, Yahya said: "This is he whom I announced." He humbly stepped aside for Isa.

chapters.title: The meeting
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And (remember) Yahya when he took the Book with strength." (19:12) Yahya and Isa met at the Jordan. Yahya recognized Isa as the Messiah. He baptized him. Yahya's mission was accomplished: he had prepared the hearts. Isa continued the message. Both prophets served Allah.

references.content: مُصَدِّقًۢا بِكَلِمَةٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَسَيِّدًۭا وَحَصُورًۭا وَنَبِيًّۭا مِّنَ ٱلْصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: Confirming a word from Allah, noble, chaste, and prophet among the righteous.
references.relevance: This verse establishes the link between Yahya and Allah's word (Isa).

references.content: يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّۭا
references.translation: O Yahya! Take the Book with strength. We gave him wisdom from childhood.

glossary.definition: Jesus in Arabic. Prophet announced by Yahya.

lessons_from_yahya.title: Lessons from the story of Yahya
lessons_from_yahya.introduction: Yahya is the prophet of purity. The Quran says: "And (Yahya) was dutiful to his parents. He was neither arrogant nor disobedient." (19:14) His life was short but exemplary. He prepared the coming of Isa and died as a martyr.
lessons_from_yahya.conclusion: Yahya lived in purity. He announced Isa. He was killed by a tyrant. But his message survived. He remains a model of piety and humility.
lessons_from_yahya.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that quality prevails over quantity. A short but pious life is worth more than a long life in sin.

chapters.title: Purity and piety
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And (Yahya) was noble, chaste, and prophet among the righteous." (3:39) Yahya aspired to nothing of this world. He lived in asceticism. He devoted himself to Allah. The believer must imitate this purity. Stay away from evil, draw closer to good.

chapters.title: Humility before truth
chapters.content: Yahya announced Isa. When Isa came, Yahya stepped aside. He said: "He must increase, and I must decrease." This humility is a lesson. The true believer does not compete. He serves the truth, whoever the messenger who carries it.

references.content: يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّۭا ۝ وَحَنَانًۭا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةًۭ ۝ وَكَانَ تَقِيًّۭا ۝ وَحَنَانًۭا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةًۭ
references.translation: O Yahya! Take the Book with strength. We gave him wisdom, tenderness and purity. He was pious.

references.content: وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَإِلْيَاسَ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And Zakariya, Yahya, Isa and Ilyas: all among the righteous.
references.relevance: This verse places Yahya among the righteous prophets.

glossary.definition: Piety, fear of Allah. Yahya was full of taqwa.
Noble, master. Yahya was sayyid, noble among men.
